page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance family server featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the system allocates 16.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU features a shared allocation model and includes 16 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. Storage is bundled with a 350 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 25% at 2869.78. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 22445.91 and a PassMark CPU score of 16668.2. Memory bandwidth is consistent, registering 84.3 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 63.29 GB/sec for write operations. The server handles database operations and web serving reliably, achieving 13.03 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 7.47 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. For machine learning tasks, LLM inference using a small 135M model reaches 192.4 tokens per second for text generation, though performance drops to a weak 16.77 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation model allows Vultr to deliver a high core count and 350 GB of fast NVMe SSD storage at a lower price tier relative to dedicated-core alternatives. However, the shared CPU resource model represents a tradeoff, as performance may fluctuate depending on co-tenant activity, making it less suitable for sustained, latency-sensitive compute tasks. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, development environments, and moderate database workloads that benefit from high local storage capacity and strong single-core burst capabilities without requiring dedicated CPU execution.
description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server delivers strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng benchmarks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation model offers a cost-effective path to high core counts and fast local storage, though it introduces potential performance variability under sustained loads. This configuration is best suited for web serving, multi-threaded development tasks, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a High Performance server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd features 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC server balancing high core counts and fast NVMe storage for cost-effective multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 350 GB of bundled NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database throughput
Cost-effective shared CPU model suitable for non-sustained peak workloads
Ideal for web serving, multi-threaded development, and moderate databases
